final_answer:
No, it does not fully match BSC right now, and yes, I missed some parity-critical details in the earlier summary.

Why it matters: without full parity on validator lifecycle details, you can ship a system that looks similar but behaves differently under load, governance actions, or validator churn.

What should be done next:
1. Run a strict parity audit (read-only) against BSC for validator creation, election cadence, active-set sizing, key model, slashing/jail, rewards, governance controls, and epoch transitions.
2. Produce a pass/fail gap table with “match / differs / missing.”
3. Only after that, lock the production implementation scope to close every “differs/missing” item.
prompt: does this match bsc chain system or not? did you miss anything or not? no is acceptable because I want this done without going on tangents
